data.title: As a DSO orchestrator, each worktree is reviewed, committed, and merged independently
data.description:
## Description

**What**: After implementation sub-agents return, orchestrator reviews and tests each worktree serially, commits in the worktree branch, then merges into session branch. Worktree retained until merge complete.
**Why**: This produces one reviewed, tested commit per worktree — smaller commits with independent validation
**Scope**:
- IN: Serial review/test loop per worktree, review sub-agent running in worktree cwd, commit in worktree (not session branch), merge worktree branch into session branch, worktree retention/cleanup, merge-state handling
- OUT: Parallel review (Epic B), Clash, conflict escalation (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, review sub-agent runs in worktree cwd and writes reviewer-findings.json to the worktree's ARTIFACTS_DIR (naturally isolated via REPO_ROOT hash)
  ← Satisfies: "orchestrator reviews and tests each worktree serially"
- When this story is complete, commit happens in the worktree branch (not session branch) — pre-commit review gate passes because review-status and diff_hash are in the same worktree ARTIFACTS_DIR
  ← Satisfies: "Each worktree that passes review produces its own commit"
- When this story is complete, merge of worktree branch into session branch succeeds and review gate skips the merge commit (merge-state.sh detects MERGE_HEAD)
  ← Satisfies: "merges into the session branch immediately"
- When this story is complete, merge-state.sh ms_get_worktree_only_files does NOT exclude the sub-agent's implementation changes during worktree-to-session merge (semantics inversion from merge-to-main handled correctly)
  ← Satisfies: "merges into the session branch immediately"
- When this story is complete, git log on session branch shows one commit per worktree with no combined batches
  ← Satisfies: "verified by git log showing one commit per worktree"
- When this story is complete, worktree is retained until merge complete; cleanup only after successful merge or explicit release (no race with Claude Code auto-cleanup)
  ← Satisfies: "Worktree retention protocol"
- When this story is complete, merge-to-main.sh runs once at session end, not per worktree merge
  ← Satisfies: "Each worktree produces its own commit"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Fresh worktree ARTIFACTS_DIR starts empty — commit MUST happen after review in same worktree context so review-status exists
- [Reliability] Worktree cleanup race: sub-agent worktrees created by isolation:worktree may have framework-managed cleanup that races with orchestrator's retention needs. Must hold worktree open (e.g., dirty sentinel file or explicit lock) until merge
- [Reliability] merge-state.sh semantics inversion: ms_get_worktree_only_files was designed for merge-to-main (exclude main's changes). During worktree-to-session merge, the 'incoming' changes ARE the ones we want. Must ensure this code path does not filter out reviewed changes
- [Testing] test-gate-status must be written in worktree context before commit

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
